heading: Treatment of income and resources for certain institutionalized spouses

L. 100–485, title VI, § 608(d)(16)(D)102 Stat. 2418July 1, 1988Oct. 13, 1988“(1)(A)The amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending sections 1382, 1382b, 1396a, 1396p, 1396r, subsection (a)) shall only apply to institutionalized individuals who begin continuous periods of institutionalization on or after , except that subsections (b) and (d) of such section (and so much of subsection (e) of such section as relates to such other subsections) shall apply as of such date to individuals institutionalized on or after such date.42 U.S.C. 1396r–5September 30, 1989“(2)(A)The amendment made by subsection (b) [amending ] and section 1902(a)(51)(B) of the Social Security Act [], apply (except as provided in paragraph (5)) to payments under title XIX of the Social Security The final rule of the Health Care Financing Administration published on (53 Federal Register 3586) is superseded to the extent inconsistent with the amendment made by subsection (d).section 1396a of this titleApril 8, 1988February 8, 1988“(5)In the case of a State plan for medical assistance under title XIX of the Social Security Act which the Secretary of Health and Human Services determines requires State legislation (other than legislation appropriating funds) in order for the plan to meet the additional requirements imposed by the amendments made by this section (other than paragraphs (1) and (5) of subsection (e) [amending ]), the State plan shall not be regarded as failing to comply with the requirements of such title solely on the basis of its failure to meet these additional requirements before the first day of the first calendar quarter beginning after the close of the first regular session of the State legislature that begins after the date of the enactment of this Act. For purposes of the previous sentence, in the case of a State that has a 2-year legislative session, each year of such session shall be deemed to be a separate regular session of the State legislature.section 1396a of this title“(6)The amendments made by paragraphs (1) and (5) of subsection (e) [amending ] shall apply to medical assistance furnished on or after .”section 1396a of this titleOctober 1, 1982

Rule of Construction, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–16, § 2(b)133 Stat. 852Apr. 18, 2019“(1).—Protecting state spousal income and asset disregard flexibility under waivers and plan amendmentsNothing in ( note) or section 1924 of the Social Security Act () shall be construed as prohibiting a State from disregarding an individual’s spousal income and assets under a State waiver or plan amendment described in paragraph (2) for purposes of making determinations of eligibility for home and community-based services or home and community-based attendant services and supports under such waiver or plan amendment.section 2404 of Public Law 111–14842 U.S.C. 1396r–542 U.S.C. 1396r–5“(2).—State waiver or plan amendment describedA State waiver or plan amendment described in this paragraph is any of the following:“(A)A waiver or plan amendment to provide medical assistance for home and community-based services under a waiver or plan amendment under subsection (c), (d), or (i) of section 1915 of the Social Security Act () or under section 1115 of such Act ().42 U.S.C. 1396n42 U.S.C. 1315“(B)A plan amendment to provide medical assistance for home and community-based services for individuals by reason of being determined eligible under section 1902(a)(10)(C) of such Act () or by reason of section 1902(f) of such Act () or otherwise on the basis of a reduction of income based on costs incurred for medical or other remedial care under which the State disregarded the income and assets of the individual’s spouse in determining the initial and ongoing financial eligibility of an individual for such services in place of the spousal impoverishment provisions applied under section 1924 of such Act ().42 U.S.C. 1396a(a)(10)(C)42 U.S.C. 1396a(f)42 U.S.C. 1396r–5“(C)A plan amendment to provide medical assistance for home and community-based attendant services and supports under section 1915(k) of such Act ().”42 U.S.C. 1396n(k)Similar provisions were contained in the following prior act:, , .Pub. L. 116–260section 1396a of this title

children.heading: Special treatment for institutionalized spouses
children.label: a Special treatment for institutionalized spouses

children.heading: Supersedes other provisions
children.label: 1 Supersedes other provisions
children.text: In determining the eligibility for medical assistance of an institutionalized spouse (as defined in subsection (h)(1)), the provisions of this section supersede any other provision of this subchapter (including sections 1396a(a)(17) and 1396a(f) of this title) which is inconsistent with them.

children.heading: No comparable treatment required
children.label: 2 No comparable treatment required
children.text: Any different treatment provided under this section for institutionalized spouses shall not, by reason of paragraph (10) or (17) of , require such treatment for other individuals.section 1396a(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the determination of what constitutes income or resources, or

children.label: B
children.text: the methodology and standards for determining and evaluating income and resources.

children.heading: Application in certain States and territories
children.label: 4 Application in certain States and territories

children.heading: Application in States operating under demonstration projects
children.label: A Application in States operating under demonstration projects
children.text: In the case of any State which is providing medical assistance to its residents under a waiver granted under , the Secretary shall require the State to meet the requirements of this section in the same manner as the State would be required to meet such requirement if the State had in effect a plan approved under this subchapter.section 1315 of this title

children.heading: No application in commonwealths and territories
children.label: B No application in commonwealths and territories
children.text: This section shall only apply to a State that is one of the 50 States or the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Application to individuals receiving services under PACE programs
children.label: 5 Application to individuals receiving services under PACE programs
children.text: This section applies to individuals receiving institutional or noninstitutional services under a PACE demonstration waiver program (as defined in ) or under a PACE program under section 1396u–4 or 1395eee of this title.section 1396u–4(a)(7) of this title

children.heading: Rules for treatment of income
children.label: b Rules for treatment of income

children.heading: Separate treatment of income
children.label: 1 Separate treatment of income
children.text: During any month in which an institutionalized spouse is in the institution, except as provided in paragraph (2), no income of the community spouse shall be deemed available to the institutionalized spouse.

children.label: i
children.text: if payment of income is made solely in the name of the institutionalized spouse or the community spouse, the income shall be considered available only to that respective spouse;

children.label: ii
children.text: if payment of income is made in the names of the institutionalized spouse and the community spouse, one-half of the income shall be considered available to each of them; and

children.label: iii
children.text: if payment of income is made in the names of the institutionalized spouse or the community spouse, or both, and to another person or persons, the income shall be considered available to each spouse in proportion to the spouse’s interest (or, if payment is made with respect to both spouses and no such interest is specified, one-half of the joint interest shall be considered available to each spouse).

children.label: i
children.text: except as provided in clause (ii), income shall be attributed in accordance with the provisions of this subchapter (including sections 1396a(a)(17) and 1396p(d) of this title), and

children.label: I
children.text: if payment of income is made solely to the institutionalized spouse or the community spouse, the income shall be considered available only to that respective spouse;

children.label: II
children.text: if payment of income is made to both the institutionalized spouse and the community spouse, one-half of the income shall be considered available to each of them; and

children.label: III
children.text: if payment of income is made to the institutionalized spouse or the community spouse, or both, and to another person or persons, the income shall be considered available to each spouse in proportion to the spouse’s interest (or, if payment is made with respect to both spouses and no such interest is specified, one-half of the joint interest shall be considered available to each spouse).

children.heading: Property with no instrument
children.label: C Property with no instrument
children.text: In the case of income not from a trust in which there is no instrument establishing ownership, subject to subparagraph (D), one-half of the income shall be considered to be available to the institutionalized spouse and one-half to the community spouse.

children.heading: Rebutting ownership
children.label: D Rebutting ownership
children.text: The rules of subparagraphs (A) and (C) are superseded to the extent that an institutionalized spouse can establish, by a preponderance of the evidence, that the ownership interests in income are other than as provided under such subparagraphs.

children.heading: Rules for treatment of resources
children.label: c Rules for treatment of resources

children.heading: Computation of spousal share at time of institutionalization
children.label: 1 Computation of spousal share at time of institutionalization
i
children.text: the total value of the resources to the extent either the institutionalized spouse or the community spouse has an ownership interest, and

children.label: ii
children.text: a spousal share which is equal to ½ of such total value.

children.heading: Assessment
children.label: B Assessment
children.text: At the request of an institutionalized spouse or community spouse, at the beginning of the first continuous period of institutionalization (beginning on or after ) of the institutionalized spouse and upon the receipt of relevant documentation of resources, the State shall promptly assess and document the total value described in subparagraph (A)(i) and shall provide a copy of such assessment and documentation to each spouse and shall retain a copy of the assessment for use under this section. If the request is not part of an application for medical assistance under this subchapter, the State may, at its option as a condition of providing the assessment, require payment of a fee not exceeding the reasonable expenses of providing and documenting the assessment. At the time of providing the copy of the assessment, the State shall include a notice indicating that the spouse will have a right to a fair hearing under subsection (e)(2).September 30, 1989

children.heading: Attribution of resources at time of initial eligibility determination
children.label: 2 Attribution of resources at time of initial eligibility determination
A
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (B), all the resources held by either the institutionalized spouse, community spouse, or both, shall be considered to be available to the institutionalized spouse, and

children.label: B
children.text: resources shall be considered to be available to an institutionalized spouse, but only to the extent that the amount of such resources exceeds the amount computed under subsection (f)(2)(A) (as of the time of application for benefits).

children.label: A
children.text: the institutionalized spouse has assigned to the State any rights to support from the community spouse;

children.label: B
children.text: the institutionalized spouse lacks the ability to execute an assignment due to physical or mental impairment but the State has the right to bring a support proceeding against a community spouse without such assignment; or

children.label: C
children.text: the State determines that denial of eligibility would work an undue hardship.

children.heading: Separate treatment of resources after eligibility for benefits established
children.label: 4 Separate treatment of resources after eligibility for benefits established
children.text: During the continuous period in which an institutionalized spouse is in an institution and after the month in which an institutionalized spouse is determined to be eligible for benefits under this subchapter, no resources of the community spouse shall be deemed available to the institutionalized spouse.

children.label: A
children.text: resources excluded under subsection (a) or (d) of , andsection 1382b of this title

children.label: B
children.text: resources that would be excluded under but for the limitation on total value described in such section.section 1382b(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Protecting income for community spouse
children.label: d Protecting income for community spouse

children.heading: Allowances to be offset from income of institutionalized spouse
children.label: 1 Allowances to be offset from income of institutionalized spouse
A
children.text: A personal needs allowance (described in ), in an amount not less than the amount specified in .section 1396a(q)(1) of this titlesection 1396a(q)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: A community spouse monthly income allowance (as defined in paragraph (2)), but only to the extent income of the institutionalized spouse is made available to (or for the benefit of) the community spouse.

children.label: C
children.text: A family allowance, for each family member, equal to at least ⅓ of the amount by which the amount described in paragraph (3)(A)(i) exceeds the amount of the monthly income of that family member.

children.label: D
children.text: Amounts for incurred expenses for medical or remedial care for the institutionalized spouse (as provided under ).section 1396a(r) of this title

children.heading: Community spouse monthly income allowance defined
children.label: 2 Community spouse monthly income allowance defined
A
children.text: except as provided in subsection (e), the minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance (established under and in accordance with paragraph (3)) for the spouse, exceeds

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of monthly income otherwise available to the community spouse (determined without regard to such an allowance).

children.heading: Establishment of minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance
children.label: 3 Establishment of minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance
i
children.text: the applicable percent (described in subparagraph (B)) of ⁄ of the income official poverty line (defined by the Office of Management and Budget and revised annually in accordance with ) for a family unit of 2 members; plus112section 9902(2) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: an excess shelter allowance (as defined in paragraph (4)).

children.label: i
children.text: , is 122 percent,September 30, 1989

children.label: ii
children.text: , is 133 percent, andJuly 1, 1991

children.label: iii
children.text: , is 150 percent.July 1, 1992

children.heading: Cap on minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance
children.label: C Cap on minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance
children.text: The minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance established under subparagraph (A) may not exceed $1,500 (subject to adjustment under subsections (e) and (g)).

children.label: A
children.text: the spouse’s expenses for rent or mortgage payment (including principal and interest), taxes and insurance and, in the case of a condominium or cooperative, required maintenance charge, for the community spouse’s principal residence, and

children.label: B
children.text: the standard utility allowance (used by the State under ) or, if the State does not use such an allowance, the spouse’s actual utility expenses,section 2014(e) of title 7

children.heading: Court ordered support
children.label: 5 Court ordered support
children.text: If a court has entered an order against an institutionalized spouse for monthly income for the support of the community spouse, the community spouse monthly income allowance for the spouse shall be not less than the amount of the monthly income so ordered.

children.heading: Application of “income first” rule to revision of community spouse resource allowance
children.label: 6 Application of “income first” rule to revision of community spouse resource allowance
children.text: For purposes of this subsection and subsections (c) and (e), a State must consider that all income of the institutionalized spouse that could be made available to a community spouse, in accordance with the calculation of the community spouse monthly income allowance under this subsection, has been made available before the State allocates to the community spouse an amount of resources adequate to provide the difference between the minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance and all income available to the community spouse.

children.label: A
children.text: a determination of eligibility for medical assistance of an institutionalized spouse, or

children.label: B
children.text: a request by either the institutionalized spouse, or the community spouse, or a representative acting on behalf of either spouse,

children.label: i
children.text: the community spouse monthly income allowance;

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of monthly income otherwise available to the community spouse (as applied under subsection (d)(2)(B));

children.label: iii
children.text: the computation of the spousal share of resources under subsection (c)(1);

children.label: iv
children.text: the attribution of resources under subsection (c)(2); or

children.label: v
children.text: the determination of the community spouse resource allowance (as defined in subsection (f)(2));

children.heading: Revision of minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance
children.label: B Revision of minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance
children.text: If either such spouse establishes that the community spouse needs income, above the level otherwise provided by the minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance, due to exceptional circumstances resulting in significant financial duress, there shall be substituted, for the minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance in subsection (d)(2)(A), an amount adequate to provide such additional income as is necessary.

children.heading: Revision of community spouse resource allowance
children.label: C Revision of community spouse resource allowance
children.text: If either such spouse establishes that the community spouse resource allowance (in relation to the amount of income generated by such an allowance) is inadequate to raise the community spouse’s income to the minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance, there shall be substituted, for the community spouse resource allowance under subsection (f)(2), an amount adequate to provide such a minimum monthly maintenance needs allowance.

children.heading: Permitting transfer of resources to community spouse
children.label: f Permitting transfer of resources to community spouse

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An institutionalized spouse may, without regard to , transfer an amount equal to the community spouse resource allowance (as defined in paragraph (2)), but only to the extent the resources of the institutionalized spouse are transferred to (or for the sole benefit of) the community spouse. The transfer under the preceding sentence shall be made as soon as practicable after the date of the initial determination of eligibility, taking into account such time as may be necessary to obtain a court order under paragraph (3).section 1396p(c)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: $12,000 (subject to adjustment under subsection (g)), or, if greater (but not to exceed the amount specified in clause (ii)(II)) an amount specified under the State plan,

children.label: ii
children.text: the lesser of (I) the spousal share computed under subsection (c)(1), or (II) $60,000 (subject to adjustment under subsection (g)),

children.label: iii
children.text: the amount established under subsection (e)(2); or

children.label: iv
children.text: the amount transferred under a court order under paragraph (3);

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of the resources otherwise available to the community spouse (determined without regard to such an allowance).

children.heading: Transfers under court orders
children.label: 3 Transfers under court orders
children.text: If a court has entered an order against an institutionalized spouse for the support of the community spouse, shall not apply to amounts of resources transferred pursuant to such order for the support of the spouse or a family member (as defined in subsection (d)(1)).section 1396p of this title

children.heading: Indexing dollar amounts
children.label: g Indexing dollar amounts
children.text: For services furnished during a calendar year after 1989, the dollar amounts specified in subsections (d)(3)(C), (f)(2)(A)(i), and (f)(2)(A)(ii)(II) shall be increased by the same percentage as the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (all items; U.S. city average) between September 1988 and the September before the calendar year involved.

children.label: A
children.text: is in a medical institution or nursing facility or who (at the option of the State) is described in , andsection 1396a(a)(10)(A)(ii)(VI) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is married to a spouse who is not in a medical institution or nursing facility;
The term “community spouse” means the spouse of an institutionalized spouse.
